element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial level designed to teach core platforming fundamentals
Shell projectile mechanic, introduces enemy complexity
Cannot be stomped, requires shell projectile

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Accelerating difficulty through world progression; individual levels oscillate between safe navigation zones and threat clusters; tension ramifies as new enemy types combine with previous mechanics

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Invisible bricks and elevated coin formations signal hidden secrets; question-mark blocks cluster indicates power-up locations
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Pipes lead to underground areas; hidden mushrooms spawn from specific brick positions; elevated brick patterns suggest coin rewards

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1: Introduction of core mechanics (jump, stomp, mushroom) with gradual threat introduction; 1-1 is pure tutorial, 1-2 adds pits, 1-3 adds fire flower and rapid enemies, 1-4 introduces first boss
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 2-4: Enemy variety increases (Bullet Bills, Lakitu, Spinies); underground/castle alternation; power-up placement becomes strategic; pattern memorization required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 5-8: Hazard density peaks; Fire Bars and Bloopers demand precision; castle levels compress threats; Worlds 7-8 loop repeats with accelerated enemy patterns; final Bowser encounter demands boss pattern mastery
notable_spikes: World 4-4: Extended Bowser castle with multiple threats
World 5-4: Fire Bar introduction and rotation complexity
World 7-2: Water level with Blooper chase mechanic
World 8-4: Final castle with compressed pit-and-Bullet Bill sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: World 1-1 isolates jumping mechanics with open space, introduces Goombas singly, then pairs. Gradually adds height variation and brick complexity. Final section teaches question-mark blocks and mushroom pickup before flagpole reward.

Type 2 levels (1-2, 2-2, etc.) transition from aboveground to subterranean pipe entry. Underground sections remove sky visibility, compress threats into narrower vertical spaces, increase pit density. Creates pacing shift from horizontal scroll to tight navigation.

Type 4 levels (1-4, 2-4, etc.) feature extended horizontal screen locks inside castle; Bowser waits at end. Early castles allow power-up collection before boss; later castles remove safe spacing and force hazard chaining. Boss pattern becomes increasingly aggressive across worlds.

Mid-to-late levels stack multiple enemy types in rapid succession (Koopa Troopas + Goombas + airborne threats). Forces player to manage multiple threat patterns simultaneously without power-up safety net. High-death-rate zones that demand memorized routing.

Levels with Fire Bars, Bullet Bills, or Bloopers arranged to create synchronized dodge windows. Screen-lock sections force player through threat field. Timing-based rather than reflex-based; rewards pattern recognition over reaction speed.
